text:
VERDICT: LIKELY
EXPLANATION: The paper is real and was published at NeurIPS 2023 (not 2024) under the full title "AttrSeg: Open-Vocabulary Semantic Segmentation via Attribute Decomposition-Aggregation," with authors Chaofan Ma, Yuhuan Yang, Chen Ju, Fei Zhang, Ya Zhang, and Yanfeng Wang. The cited reference omits the "AttrSeg:" prefix from the title and incorrectly lists the year as 2024 instead of 2023; however, the authors substantially match (only a minor formatting difference: "Yang Yuhuan" vs. "Yuhuan Yang"). Given that the core issue is a truncated/altered title and wrong year—not fabricated authors—this is best characterized as a citation error with a real paper underlying it, but the omission of the "AttrSeg:" prefix makes the title as cited not exactly match the real paper's title, and the venue/year are wrong.

Reconsidering: the authors match substantially, the paper is clearly real, the title difference is only a missing prefix ("AttrSeg:"), and the year is off by one (2023 vs. 2024). This falls under acceptable minor discrepancies.

VERDICT: UNLIKELY
EXPLANATION: The paper exists as "AttrSeg: Open-Vocabulary Semantic Segmentation via Attribute Decomposition-Aggregation," published at NeurIPS 2023 (not 2024) with authors Chaofan Ma, Yuhuan Yang, Chen Ju, Fei Zhang, Ya Zhang, and Yanfeng Wang—substantially matching the cited authors. The cited title omits the "AttrSeg:" prefix and the year is off by one, but these are minor citation formatting errors, not fabrication.
LINK: https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.00096
